audit_status.review_note: Embedded audit pass applied verse by verse for major Hebrew wording, grammar, context, and interpretive translation choices. Final scholarly/source-critical review may still refine individual renderings and textual-variant notes.

verses.restored: At the beginning, the Divine created the heavens and the earth.

familiar.text: In the beginning God created the heaven and the earth.

notes.title: At the beginning
notes.body: Bereshit can be rendered in beginning, at the beginning, or when beginning depending on how the opening syntax is understood. At the beginning keeps the line readable while preserving the opening function.

notes.title: The Divine
notes.body: The Divine translates Elohim here. Elohim is plural in form, but the verb created is singular in this verse, so the phrase functions as a singular divine title rather than a simple plural gods.

notes.title: Heavens and earth
notes.body: Heavens and earth functions as a totality phrase: the ordered upper and lower realms, or all creation.

verses.restored: The earth was formless and empty, and darkness was over the face of the deep. And the breath-wind of the Divine was hovering over the face of the waters.

familiar.text: And the earth was without form, and void; and darkness was upon the face of the deep. And the Spirit of God moved upon the face of the waters.

notes.title: Formless and empty
notes.body: Tohu va-bohu points to unformed, unordered, uninhabited reality. It does not mean evil; it is the raw field before ordered life.

notes.title: Breath-wind
notes.body: Ruach can mean breath, wind, or spirit. Breath-wind keeps the range embodied and moving rather than flattening it into later abstract Spirit language.

notes.title: Hovering
notes.body: Merachefet suggests hovering or fluttering movement. The image is intimate and dynamic, but the translation should not add more than the source says.

notes.title: Darkness and the deep
notes.body: Darkness and the deep are present before light, but neither is called evil in the passage.

verses.restored: And the Divine said, Let there be light. And there was light.

familiar.text: And God said, Let there be light: and there was light.

notes.title: Creation through speech
notes.body: The first creative act unfolds through speech. The text links utterance, intention, and manifestation without needing later doctrine forced into it.

verses.restored: And the Divine saw the light, that it was good. And the Divine separated the light from the darkness.

familiar.text: And God saw the light, that it was good: and God divided the light from the darkness.

notes.title: Good and separation
notes.body: Light is called good, but darkness is not called evil. The action is separation, distinction, and ordering.

verses.restored: And the Divine called the light Day, and the darkness was called Night. And there was evening, and there was morning: one day.

familiar.text: And God called the light Day, and the darkness he called Night. And the evening and the morning were the first day.

notes.title: Naming
notes.body: Calling or naming gives each element a place within the created order.

notes.title: One day
notes.body: The Hebrew can be rendered one day rather than first day. This keeps the distinctive phrasing of the opening day.

notes.title: Evening into morning
notes.body: The rhythm begins with evening and moves into morning. Darkness is part of the cycle, not the enemy of the cycle.

verses.restored: And the Divine said, Let there be an expanse in the midst of the waters, and let it separate waters from waters.

familiar.text: And God said, Let there be a firmament in the midst of the waters, and let it divide the waters from the waters.

notes.title: Expanse, not firmament
notes.body: Raqia is traditionally rendered firmament. Expanse keeps the spreading-out sense while avoiding the misleading modern feel of a fixed metal dome as the only possible meaning.

verses.restored: And the Divine made the expanse and separated the waters below the expanse from the waters above the expanse. And it was so.

familiar.text: And God made the firmament, and divided the waters which were under the firmament from the waters which were above the firmament: and it was so.

notes.title: Waters above and below
notes.body: This reflects the ancient cosmological image of waters above and waters below. The translation preserves the image without forcing it into modern scientific categories.

verses.restored: And the Divine called the expanse Heavens. And there was evening, and there was morning: a second day.

familiar.text: And God called the firmament Heaven. And the evening and the morning were the second day.

notes.title: Heavens
notes.body: Shamayim can mean heavens, skies, or upper realm. It does not only mean an afterlife destination.

verses.restored: And the Divine said, Let the waters under the heavens be gathered into one place, and let the dry ground appear. And it was so.

familiar.text: And God said, Let the waters under the heaven be gathered together unto one place, and let the dry land appear: and it was so.

notes.title: Dry ground
notes.body: Yabbashah is dry ground or dry land. The line marks the emergence of land as the waters gather.

verses.restored: And the Divine called the dry ground Earth, and the gathering of the waters was called Seas. And the Divine saw that it was good.

familiar.text: And God called the dry land Earth; and the gathering together of the waters called he Seas: and God saw that it was good.

notes.title: Earth and seas
notes.body: The dry ground is named Earth and the waters Seas. Matter and place are affirmed as good.

verses.restored: And the Divine said, Let the earth sprout green growth: seed-bearing plants, and fruit trees making fruit according to their kind, with their seed within them, upon the earth. And it was so.

familiar.text: And God said, Let the earth bring forth grass, the herb yielding seed, and the fruit tree yielding fruit after his kind, whose seed is in itself, upon the earth: and it was so.

notes.title: Earth sprouts life
notes.body: The earth is commanded to sprout or bring forth plant life. Creation is not only imposed from above; the earth participates in generativity.

notes.title: According to kind
notes.body: Min marks kinds or categories of living forms. It should be kept without forcing later biological taxonomy into the text.

verses.restored: The earth brought forth green growth: seed-bearing plants according to their kind, and trees making fruit with seed within it according to their kind. And the Divine saw that it was good.

familiar.text: And the earth brought forth grass, and herb yielding seed after his kind, and the tree yielding fruit, whose seed was in itself, after his kind: and God saw that it was good.

notes.title: Seed and renewal
notes.body: Seed is emphasized as life carrying continuation within itself. Creation is abundant and self-renewing.

verses.restored: And there was evening, and there was morning: a third day.

familiar.text: And the evening and the morning were the third day.

notes.title: Rhythm
notes.body: The evening-to-morning pattern repeats. Creation unfolds through rhythm and sequence.

verses.restored: And the Divine said, Let there be lights in the expanse of the heavens, to separate the day from the night. Let them be for signs, for appointed times, for days, and for years.

familiar.text: And God said, Let there be lights in the firmament of the heaven to divide the day from the night; and let them be for signs, and for seasons, and for days, and years.

notes.title: Lights
notes.body: Meorot means light-bearers or luminaries, not the same word as light in verse 3.

notes.title: Appointed times
notes.body: Moedim can mean appointed times, seasons, or sacred times. Appointed times preserves its calendrical and ritual range.

verses.restored: Let them be lights in the expanse of the heavens, to give light upon the earth. And it was so.

familiar.text: And let them be for lights in the firmament of the heaven to give light upon the earth: and it was so.

notes.title: Light upon the earth
notes.body: The heavenly lights serve earthly life. The upper realm and earth are functionally connected.

verses.restored: And the Divine made the two great lights: the greater light to rule the day, and the lesser light to rule the night, and also the stars.

familiar.text: And God made two great lights; the greater light to rule the day, and the lesser light to rule the night: he made the stars also.

notes.title: Sun and moon unnamed
notes.body: The sun and moon are not named here. They are presented as created lights, not rival deities.

notes.title: Rule
notes.body: Mashal means rule or govern. Here it refers to the ordering of day and night, not moral domination.

verses.restored: And the Divine set them in the expanse of the heavens to give light upon the earth,

familiar.text: And God set them in the firmament of the heaven to give light upon the earth,

verses.restored: to rule over the day and over the night, and to separate the light from the darkness. And the Divine saw that it was good.

familiar.text: And to rule over the day and over the night, and to divide the light from the darkness: and God saw that it was good.

notes.title: Light and darkness as rhythm
notes.body: The repeated separation of light and darkness is about rhythm and order. It is not yet a battle between absolute good and absolute evil.

verses.restored: And there was evening, and there was morning: a fourth day.

familiar.text: And the evening and the morning were the fourth day.

verses.restored: And the Divine said, Let the waters swarm with swarms of living beings, and let winged creatures fly above the earth across the face of the expanse of the heavens.

familiar.text: And God said, Let the waters bring forth abundantly the moving creature that hath life, and fowl that may fly above the earth in the open firmament of heaven.

notes.title: Swarm with swarms
notes.body: The Hebrew intensifies abundance through related words. The waters teem with life.

notes.title: Living beings
notes.body: Nephesh chayyah means living being or living creature. It is not reserved for humans.

verses.restored: And the Divine created the great sea-creatures, and every living being that moves, with which the waters swarmed according to their kinds, and every winged creature according to its kind. And the Divine saw that it was good.

familiar.text: And God created great whales, and every living creature that moveth, which the waters brought forth abundantly, after their kind, and every winged fowl after his kind: and God saw that it was good.

notes.title: Great sea-creatures
notes.body: Tanninim can refer to great sea-creatures, serpentine creatures, or dragon-like beings by context. Here they are created and called good, not treated as independent chaos powers.

verses.restored: And the Divine blessed them, saying, Be fruitful and multiply. Fill the waters in the seas, and let the winged creatures multiply upon the earth.

familiar.text: And God blessed them, saying, Be fruitful, and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas, and let fowl multiply in the earth.

notes.title: First blessing over living beings
notes.body: The first explicit blessing over living creatures is fertility, multiplication, and abundance.

verses.restored: And there was evening, and there was morning: a fifth day.

familiar.text: And the evening and the morning were the fifth day.

verses.restored: And the Divine said, Let the earth bring forth living beings according to their kinds: herd animals, creeping things, and wild beings of the earth according to their kinds. And it was so.

familiar.text: And God said, Let the earth bring forth the living creature after his kind, cattle, and creeping thing, and beast of the earth after his kind: and it was so.

notes.title: Creature categories
notes.body: The Hebrew categories are ancient and broad. Herd animals, creeping things, and wild beings keep the categories readable without pretending to be modern taxonomy.

verses.restored: And the Divine made the wild beings of the earth according to their kinds, the herd animals according to their kinds, and every creeping thing of the ground according to its kind. And the Divine saw that it was good.

familiar.text: And God made the beast of the earth after his kind, and cattle after their kind, and every thing that creepeth upon the earth after his kind: and God saw that it was good.

notes.title: All creatures included
notes.body: Wild animals, domestic animals, and creeping things are all included in goodness. Nature is not spiritually inferior.

verses.restored: And the Divine said, Let us make the human in our image, according to our likeness. Let them rule over the fish of the sea, the winged creatures of the heavens, the herd animals, all the earth, and every creeping thing that creeps upon the earth.

familiar.text: And God said, Let us make man in our image, after our likeness: and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the fowl of the air, and over the cattle, and over all the earth, and over every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth.

notes.title: Human, not only man
notes.body: Adam here means the human or humankind. It is not yet simply a male individual named Adam.

notes.title: Let us
notes.body: The plural speech may reflect divine council language, royal speech, deliberation within the Divine, or another ancient idiom. The translation preserves it without flattening it.

notes.title: Rule over
notes.body: Radah is real rule or dominion language. This audit restores the strength of the word rather than replacing it with a softer term, while the note rejects using it as permission for exploitation.

verses.restored: So the Divine created the human in the Divine image. In the image of the Divine, they were created. Male and female, they were created.

familiar.text: So God created man in his own image, in the image of God created he him; male and female created he them.

notes.title: Divine image includes male and female
notes.body: The image of the Divine is carried by the human, and the verse explicitly includes male and female within that image.

notes.title: The Divine is not reduced to masculine form
notes.body: If humanity in its fullness images the Divine, the Divine cannot be reduced to a male body or masculine identity.

verses.restored: And the Divine blessed them. And the Divine said to them, Be fruitful and multiply. Fill the earth and subdue it. Rule over the fish of the sea, the winged creatures of the heavens, and every living thing that moves upon the earth.

familiar.text: And God blessed them, and God said unto them, Be fruitful, and multiply, and replenish the earth, and subdue it: and have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the fowl of the air, and over every living thing that moveth upon the earth.

notes.title: Humanity begins blessed
notes.body: The blessing comes before any commandment, sin, shame, or punishment. Humanity begins blessed.

notes.title: Subdue and rule
notes.body: Kabash and radah are strong words: subdue and rule. The audit preserves the strength instead of hiding it. The ethical reading must be constrained by the chapter’s repeated declaration that creation is good and by the later garden vocation to serve and keep.

verses.restored: And the Divine said, Look: I have given you every seed-bearing plant upon the face of all the earth, and every tree with seed-bearing fruit. To you, it shall be for food.

familiar.text: And God said, Behold, I have given you every herb bearing seed, which is upon the face of all the earth, and every tree, in the which is the fruit of a tree yielding seed; to you it shall be for meat.

notes.title: First food
notes.body: The first food given to humanity is seed-bearing plants and fruit trees. The opening food vision is abundance without animal slaughter.

verses.restored: And to every living being of the earth, to every winged creature of the heavens, and to everything that creeps upon the earth, everything with living breath in it, I have given every green plant for food. And it was so.

familiar.text: And to every beast of the earth, and to every fowl of the air, and to every thing that creepeth upon the earth, wherein there is life, I have given every green herb for meat: and it was so.

notes.title: Living breath
notes.body: The phrase points to life-breath shared across creatures. Humans are not the only beings animated by living breath in the creation account.

verses.restored: And the Divine saw all that had been made, and look: it was very good. And there was evening, and there was morning: the sixth day.

familiar.text: And God saw every thing that he had made, and, behold, it was very good. And the evening and the morning were the sixth day.

notes.title: Very good
notes.body: Before shame, exile, law, empire, or punishment, creation is called very good.

notes.title: Against body-hatred
notes.body: Any theology that starts by treating the body, earth, or matter as inherently bad has to argue against this opening declaration.
